name: Amazon Conversions Api
description: Amazon conversion API destination to send conversion event data to Amazon.

region.label: Region
region.description: Region for API Endpoint, either NA, EU, FE.

advertiserId.label: Amazon Advertiser ID
advertiserId.description: Your Amazon Advertiser Account ID. This must be a numeric value. Use Amazon DSP CFID and not Entity ID.

dataSetName.label: Dataset Name
dataSetName.description: Amazon Ads organizes uploaded data into datasets, which are logical groupings used to separate and categorize events from your sources. The default dataset name (if not provided) is Default_Events. All events within a dataset will appear in Amazon Ads Data Manager under the name you provide here. New destination? We recommend providing a dataset name during initial setup. Existing destination? We strongly recommend reading the [FAQ](https://www.twilio.com/docs/segment/connections/destinations/catalog/actions-amazon-conversions-api#what-is-a-dataset-and-how-does-amazon-use-the-dataset-name) before updating your dataset name, as changes may impact your existing events.

trackConversion.title: Track Conversion
trackConversion.description: Send conversion event data to Amazon Events API

name.label: Event Name
name.description: The name of the imported event.

eventActionSource.label: Event Action Source
eventActionSource.description: The platform from which the event was sourced. If no value is provided, then website is used as default.

countryCode.label: Country Code
countryCode.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. e.g., US, GB. Also accepts locale codes. e.g en-US, en-GB.

timestamp.label: Event Timestamp
timestamp.description: The reported timestamp of when the event occurred in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssTZD).

currencyCode.label: Currency Code
currencyCode.description: The currencyCode associated with the 'value' of the event in ISO-4217 format. Only applicable for OFF_AMAZON_PURCHASES event type.

unitsSold.label: Units Sold
unitsSold.description: The number of items purchased. Only applicable for OFF_AMAZON_PURCHASES event type. If not provided on the event, a default of 1 will be applied.

clientDedupeId.label: Client Dedupe ID
clientDedupeId.description: Amazon Conversions API uses the `clientDedupeId` field to prevent duplicate events. By default, Segment maps the messageId to this field. For events with the same clientDedupeId, only the latest event will be processed. Please be advised that deduplication occurs across all event types, rather than being limited to individual event types.

matchKeys.label: Match Keys
matchKeys.description: Match keys are used to identify the customer associated with the event for attribution. At least one match key must be provided.

email.label: Email
email.description: Customer email address associated with the event.

phone.label: Phone Number
phone.description: Customer phone number associated with the event.

firstName.label: First Name
firstName.description: Customer first name associated with the event.

lastName.label: Last Name
lastName.description: Customer last name associated with the event.

address.label: Address
address.description: Customer address associated with the event.

city.label: City
city.description: Customer city associated with the event.

state.label: State
state.description: Customer state associated with the event.

postalCode.label: Postal Code
postalCode.description: Customer postal code associated with the event.

maid.label: Mobile Ad ID
maid.description: Mobile advertising ID (MAID). ADID, IDFA, or FIREADID can be passed into this field.

rampId.label: RAMP ID
rampId.description: RAMP ID for the customer. Used for attribution to traffic events.

matchId.label: Match ID
matchId.description: Match ID serves as an anonymous, opaque unique identifier that corresponds to individual users within an advertiser system, such as loyalty membership identifications and order references. This functionality enables advertisers to precisely monitor campaign effectiveness while maintaining customer data privacy, eliminating the need to share sensitive information like hashed email addresses or phone numbers with Amazon, particularly when analyzing complex customer journeys across multiple channels and devices. The advertisers who implement the Amazon Advertising Tag (AAT) on their websites can transmit match_id as a parameter in conjunction with online event tracking. The Amazon system subsequently correlates these identifiers with users through cookies or hashed Personally Identifiable Information (PII). In instances where users complete offline conversions, advertisers can report these activities through the Conversions API (CAPI) utilizing the corresponding match_id, ensuring seamless cross-channel attribution.

dataProcessingOptions.label: Data Processing Options
dataProcessingOptions.description: A list of flags for signaling how an event shall be processed. Events marked for limited data use will not be processed.

consent.label: Consent
consent.description: Describes consent given by the user for advertising purposes. For EU advertisers, it is required to provide one of Geo ipAddress, amazonConsent, tcf, or gpp.

ipAddress.label: Geographic Consent: IP Address
ipAddress.description: Captures the user's geographic information (IP address) for consent checking.

amznAdStorage.label: Ad Storage Consent
amznAdStorage.description: Amazon Consent Format: Captures whether the user has consented to cookie based tracking.

amznUserData.label: User Data Consent
amznUserData.description: Amazon Consent Format: Captures whether the user has consented to use personal data for advertising.

tcf.label: TCF String
tcf.description: An encoded Transparency and Consent Framework (TCF) string describing user consent choices.

gpp.label: GPP String
gpp.description: An encoded Global Privacy Platform (GPP) string describing user privacy preferences.

customAttributes.label: Custom Attributes
customAttributes.description: Custom attributes associated with the event to provide additional context. Note that only brand, category, productId and attr1 - attr10 custom attributes are used for reporting.

brand.label: Brand
brand.description: The brand associated with the event.

category.label: Category
category.description: The category associated with the event.

productId.label: Product ID
productId.description: The product ID associated with the event.

attr1.label: Attribute 1
attr1.description: Custom attribute 1 associated with the event.

attr2.label: Attribute 2
attr2.description: Custom attribute 2 associated with the event.

attr3.label: Attribute 3
attr3.description: Custom attribute 3 associated with the event.

attr4.label: Attribute 4
attr4.description: Custom attribute 4 associated with the event.

attr5.label: Attribute 5
attr5.description: Custom attribute 5 associated with the event.

attr6.label: Attribute 6
attr6.description: Custom attribute 6 associated with the event.

attr7.label: Attribute 7
attr7.description: Custom attribute 7 associated with the event.

attr8.label: Attribute 8
attr8.description: Custom attribute 8 associated with the event.

attr9.label: Attribute 9
attr9.description: Custom attribute 9 associated with the event.

attr10.label: Attribute 10
attr10.description: Custom attribute 10 associated with the event.

enable_batching.label: Enable Batching
enable_batching.description: When enabled, Segment will send data in batching.

batch_size.label: Batch Size
batch_size.description: Maximum number of events to include in each batch. Actual batch sizes may be lower.
